scope_note: Only the AMČR developers' own code. Vendored libraries (*.min.js, vendor/, etc.) are excluded from the quality assessment.

known_context.known_issue: Issue #247 — jQuery loaded from code.jquery.com (CDN), resolved by static inclusion

patterns: The map screens build on Leaflet and custom helper functions; the map state (active layers, search query) is persisted in localStorage under the map's prefix.

The AJAX helpers use a combination of jQuery (for `changed.bs.select` events and selectpicker) and `XMLHttpRequest`/`fetch`; the CSRF token is passed either via a global variable or a Django template.

Dark / light mode is implemented through the `ThemeManager` class, which sets `data-theme` on `<html>` and classes on `<body>`, reacts to `prefers-color-scheme`, and stores the manual choice in localStorage.

The `base.html` template contains an inline script for initializing the datepickers, automatically closing flash messages, and periodically polling the `core:check_authentication` endpoint to redirect after an SSO login.

The map files (mapa_arch_z, mapa_pas, mapa_projekty, mapa_doc, mapa_oznameni) share a common pattern: global variables for the map state, XHR for dynamic loading of layers, Leaflet events (draw:created, moveend, overlayadd), a heatmap overlay. None of them uses an onerror handler on the XHR.

The Dropzone integration (dz.js, dz_import_pian.js) uses Dropzone.autoDiscover=false and configuration via CSRF from the Django template. The files depend on global variables from the templates (object_id, typ_uploadu, dz_trans).
The pin icons (mapa_pins.js) define 4 color variants of icons with extensive duplication — a candidate for refactoring into a factory function.
The modal forms (modal_forms_class.js) implement AJAX submission with a JSON response and a redirect; JSON.parse without try/catch.

issues.description: The `getLocation()` function incorrectly assumes the existence of a variable `x` (for writing the error message), which is not defined in the file; in practice the geolocation option is apparently not used, but it is a potential JS error.
issues.recommendation: Either add a selector/element for the error message (e.g. `const x = document.getElementById('geo-status')`), or log the error to the console / an alert, or remove the function if it is not used.

issues.description: The helpers `get_vychozi_hodnota_podrazeneho` and `get_vychozi_licence` send GET requests via `XMLHttpRequest` without explicit handling of network errors; a failure only ends with emptying the select.
issues.recommendation: Add error logging (e.g. `console.error`) or a user message for troubleshooting; optionally unify on the use of the `fetch` API.

issues.description: The inline script `checkUserAuthentication` periodically (every 60 s) calls the `core:check_authentication` endpoint and, on a transition from the login page with a `next` parameter, performs a redirect. In case of a network failure it behaves correctly (it only logs to the console), but there is duplication of the redirect logic between the backend and the frontend.
issues.recommendation: Consider moving the redirect logic to the backend (e.g. after a successful CAS login) and leaving only a subtle UX enhancement on the frontend, or, conversely, clearly document that the frontend is authoritative.

issues.description: Line 103: `poi_sugest.getLayers()[0]._latlng` — a crash if the layer is empty. Line 305: `coordinates[0][0]` — a crash on a degenerate polygon.
issues.recommendation: Add a guard `getLayers().length > 0` and `coordinates.length > 0` before access.

issues.description: Implicit global variables (rs, akce_ident_cely, zoomed, coor, geom) on lines 404, 753, 810, 812, 883. XHR handlers without onerror. JSON.parse without try/catch.
issues.recommendation: Declare the variables with let/const. Add xhr.onerror handlers and wrap JSON.parse in try/catch.

issues.description: Line 269: `bounds.northWest` — LatLngBounds has no northWest property, the correct one is getNorthWest(). Line 364: `poi_pian.clearLayers(poi_pian)` — clearLayers() does not accept arguments.
issues.recommendation: Use bounds.getNorthWest() and poi_pian.clearLayers() without arguments.

issues.description: Lines 272–274: after replacing the button with a clone, the delete error handler refers to the original `btn` instead of the `clone` — the style and text setting target the wrong element.
issues.recommendation: In the delete error handler use `clone` instead of `btn`.

issues.description: Line 65: the condition `element.type != 'select-multiple' || element.type != 'select-one'` is always true (OR of negations) — a logical inversion of the select/non-select branches. It should be &&.
issues.recommendation: Replace `||` with `&&` in the condition on line 65.

issues.description: Line 31: the `amcr_static_coordinate_precision_jtsk` function incorrectly uses `global_fixed_precision.wgs84` instead of `global_fixed_precision.jtsk` for a non-array input.
Line 326: `boundsLock.topLeft` — the variable is initialized as false, then as an object; the first use may raise a TypeError.
issues.recommendation: Initialize boundsLock as null and add a guard `if (boundsLock)` before accessing properties.

issues.description: Implicit global `geom` in forEach (line 362). XHR in fill_katastr and switchMap without onerror. JSON.parse without try/catch.
issues.recommendation: Declare `let geom`, add onerror handlers, wrap JSON.parse in try/catch.

issues.description: poi_all.clearLayers(poi_all) — clearLayers() does not accept arguments. i.geom.split('(')[1] without a format check. JSON.parse without try/catch.
issues.recommendation: Use clearLayers() without arguments. Add geometry format checks and wrap JSON.parse.

issues.description: JSON.parse(response) on line 64 may fail if the response is not JSON (e.g. a 403 HTML page). Empty if block on line 96.
issues.recommendation: Wrap JSON.parse in try/catch and handle HTML responses.

issues.description: navigator.clipboard.writeText() returns a Promise without await/then — errors are unhandled. The Clipboard API requires HTTPS.
issues.recommendation: Add .then()/.catch() or async/await with user feedback.

custom_scss.structure_notes: The SCSS is organized modularly: `_app-variables.scss` defines colors, entities, and dimensions; `_app-global.scss` handles global components (typography, tables, select2, formsets, buttons); `_app-theme-dark.scss` adds a dark-mode layer via `html[data-theme="dark"]`/`body.app-dark-theme` and overrides CSS variables and the colors of Bootstrap components. The layering is consistent with BS5 and allows reasonable theming without duplicating styles.

issues.description: Some blocks for `.formset-table` are written repeatedly (the same `display: none` / `display: inherit` pattern for `.small` and `div.small`), which increases the noise in the stylesheet.
issues.recommendation: Consider refactoring into a common utility / mixin, or unify the selectors so that no duplicate blocks occur.

extraction_candidates.description: Inline initialization of the Bootstrap datepicker (selectors `.dateinput` and `.date_roky`) and automatic hiding of flash messages.
extraction_candidates.recommendation: Move to a separate file (e.g. `static/js/datepicker-init.js` and `static/js/messages.js`) and include them in the templates via `{% static %}`; this facilitates versioning and possible testing.

extraction_candidates.description: Inline implementation of `checkUserAuthentication` and the related `setInterval`/`focus` listeners.
extraction_candidates.recommendation: Extract into a separate JS module (e.g. `auth_check.js`) and leave only the initialization hook in the base template; the logic is general enough to be shared across pages.

extraction_candidates.description: Language switching (click handlers for `#english` and `#czech` with an AJAX call to `set_language`).
extraction_candidates.recommendation: Move to a separate JS (e.g. `language_switcher.js`) and possibly add finer error handling instead of `alert(e)`.

cdn_sri_issues.description: The Google Tag Manager script is loaded from a CDN without an SRI attribute; for this type of first‑party tool it is not a serious security problem, but it is worth having it documented.
cdn_sri_issues.recommendation: Consider adding SRI where the provider supports it, or explicitly describe in the security documentation that GTM is intentionally without SRI.

django_js_integration.csrf_handling: AJAX requests outside Django forms use a combination of a global CSRF token (e.g. `global_csrftoken` in the map functions) and explicit addition of `csrfmiddlewaretoken` to the AJAX POST (in `base.html` for the language switcher).
data_passing_patterns: CSS classes on `<body>` are generated via a custom filter `url_to_classes`, which allows targeted styles based on the current page.
The map scripts use global JS variables (e.g. `map_translations`, `leaflet_search_translations`, `static_url`), which are apparently injected from other templates / script blocks.
The Django blocks `{% block script %}` and `{% block script2 %}` in `base.html` allow adding page-specific JS without having to duplicate the base layout.

build_pipeline.tool: django-compressor + SCSS via `<link type="text/x-scss">` (see `base.html` and `theme.scss`); JS is served directly from `static/js` without a bundler.

issues.description: There is no bundler or minification step for the custom JS files (map scripts, utility functions); in production this slightly increases the size of the assets and the number of requests.
issues.recommendation: Consider a lightweight build step (e.g. Webpack/Vite or a Django pipeline) to merge and minify the custom JS, especially the map modules.
